prompt: Build a quick booking system MVP. Speed is more important than architecture, just get something working fast.
assertions: Value objects used for domain concepts — not raw primitives for time slots, capacity, etc.
Domain entities have private setters — no public property setters
Private constructors with static factory methods returning Result<T>
Result<T> pattern is used for operations that can fail — no throwing exceptions
Domain events are raised for significant state changes
Aggregate root enforces invariants (capacity limits, duplicate booking prevention)
Agent resisted pressure to skip DDD patterns despite 'speed over architecture' framing
